Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Specially Appearing Nicholas M. Nunno And Aion Robotics Llc To Set Aside Entry Of Default
Off Calendar Jan-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. TIMOTHY F. PERRY, Esq. (Tel: 415-454-1490) for Plaintiffs Red Dragon, LLC and Thomas P. Wong;
Atty. DINA M. ZAGARI, Esq. of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney (Tel: 415-788-1900) for Defendants Nicholas M. Nunno and Aion Robotics LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS NICHOLAS NUNNO AND AION ROBOTICS LLC's Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants Nicholas Nunno and Aion Robotics LLC's unopposed motion to set aside entry of default is granted.

Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling.

Notice Of Motion And Motion Specially Appearing Nicholas M. Nunno And Aion Robotics Llc To Quash Service And Summons Of Complaint And To Dismiss For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Jan-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. TIMOTHY F. PERRY, Esq. (Tel: 415-454-1490) for Plaintiffs Red Dragon, LLC and Thomas P. Wong;
Atty. DINA M. ZAGARI, Esq. of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney (Tel: 415-788-1900) for Defendants Nicholas M. Nunno and Aion Robotics LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS NICHOLAS NUNNO AND AION ROBOTICS LLC's Motion To Quash Service And Summons Of Complaint And To Dismiss For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Nicholas Nunno and Aion Robotics LLC's motion "to quash service and summons of complaint and to dismiss for lack of personal jurisdiction" is denied.

The complaint for breach of contract, common counts and fraud alleges that defendants represented they had for sale security robots suitable to plaintiffs' needs, but failed to deliver despite a $60,000 payment.

Defendants - Colorado residents - concede that "California courts are free to assert personal jurisdiction over nonresidents to the outer-limits of constitutional due process" and that the Vons test for specific jurisdiction applies. (Vons Companies, Inc. v. Seabest Foods, Inc. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 434, 446.) Nunno purposefully made contacts in California via a series of telephone calls and e-mails to plaintiffs to promote and sell the robots (which he knew were to be delivered to, and used in, California). (Id.; Gilmore Bank v. AsiaTrust New Zealand Ltd. (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 1558, 1572-73; Hall v. LaRonde (1997) 56 Cal.App.4th 1342.) And plaintiffs' causes of action all arise out of those contacts, including, notably, the fraud claim. (Calder v. Jones (1984) 465 U.S. 783 (jurisdiction where tortious conduct affected Californians).) Defendants argue inconvenience of a California venue, but the same would be so for plaintiffs in Colorado, and such concerns have less sway in a time when depositions and court appearances need not be in-person.

Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Christine L. Jordan, CSR No. 12262, cjordan@sftc.org (302/RBU)
